+---
+## What
+Complete the selection tier per plan.md ┬¦6/┬¦7 (replaces Multitool's `lexicallyFilter` keep/drop):
+- Over budget (prefix > `capacityCharacterLimit`): retrieval tier ranks the catalog, top-M candidates (default 24, best-first ordering passed to the prompt) seed a **one-off session** ŌĆö no caching, no fork
+- Id-enum grammar for the one-off session is constrained to the top-M candidate ids only
+- Report the cut via `onRetrievalCut` callback event (mirror Multitool's `onPrefilterCut` pattern, `Librarian.swift:49,88,204`: items considered, items cut, default logs)
+- `.auto` mode: selection when a model/session factory is configured, else retrieval (plan.md ┬¦7)
+- Retrieval-tier `signals` attach to over-budget results where retrieval participated
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A catalog whose assembled prefix exceeds the budget takes the one-off path: no root caching, zero forks, candidate count == min(candidateLimit, catalog size)
+- [ ] `onRetrievalCut` fires with accurate counts; under-budget searches never fire it
+- [ ] `.auto` resolves to selection with a session factory configured and retrieval without one
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistryTests/OverBudgetTests.swift` ŌĆö scripted fakes: budget boundary (at/over limit), top-M membership and ordering, one-off grammar id set, cut-event payload, `.auto` resolution both ways
+- [ ] Run `swift test` ŌĆö all pass, no GPU
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

Extract the tie-breaking sort logic into a private static helper: `private static func sortByNormalizedScore(_ indices: [Int], using normalized: [Int: Double]) -> [Int]` and call from both locations.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry/MetadataSearcher.swift:230` ŌĆö This sorting comparator duplicates the identical logic from retrievalSearch (line ~189) ŌĆö both normalize then sort by descending fused score with identical tie-breaking. Duplicate sorting code inflates maintenance surface area. Call the extracted static helper function instead of repeating the sort comparator.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry/MetadataSearcher.swift:356` ŌĆö Identical error-handling pattern appears three times in computeCosineRanking (lines 356, 368, 375) ŌĆö each reports .embeddingUnavailable and returns the same zero-filled ranking. Rule of three: extract into a shared helper function. Extract to a private helper method that encapsulates the embedding-unavailable error response, or restructure to call a common error-return path.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry/MetadataSearcher.swift:375` ŌĆö This error handling duplicates the identical pattern from lines 356 and 368 ŌĆö same onDiagnostic(.embeddingUnavailable) and return ([], zeroScores), repeated three times in computeCosineRanking. Call extracted helper function for this error case.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry/MetadataSearcher.swift:418` ŌĆö Function name `computeBM25Ranking` uses uppercase 'BM25' while similar algorithm functions use lowercase algorithm names: `computeTrigramRanking`, `computeCosineRanking`. The corresponding property names also use lowercase: `bm25Ranking`, `trigramRanking`, `cosineRanking`. This inconsistency breaks the established naming pattern for algorithm-prefixed identifiers within the file. Rename `computeBM25Ranking` to `computeBm25Ranking` to match the lowercase algorithm-name pattern established by `computeTrigramRanking` and `computeCosineRanking`.
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsMetadataRegistry/MetadataSearcher.swift:437` ŌĆö The expression `[Double](repeating: 0.0, count: index.count)` appears three times across signal-ranking functions when a signal cannot be computed (empty query, disabled weight, missing embedder). This repeated initialization pattern should be extracted to a named helper to eliminate duplication and clarify semantics. Extract to a private static helper: `private static func zeroScoresArray(count: Int) -> [Double] { [Double](repeating: 0.0, count: count) }` and replace all three occurrences with calls to this helper.
